heading: Agricultural land easements
label: 3865b Agricultural land easements

children.text: the purchase by eligible entities of agricultural land easements in eligible land;
technical assistance to implement the program, including technical assistance for the development of a conservation plan under subsection (b)(4)(C)(iv); and
buy-protect-sell transactions.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall protect the agricultural use, including grazing, and related conservation values of eligible land through cost-share assistance to eligible entities for purchasing agricultural land easements.

children.label: i
children.text: the Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice;

children.label: ii
children.text: an areawide market analysis or survey; or

children.label: iii
children.text: another industry-approved method.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Under the agreement, the eligible entity shall provide a share that is at least equivalent to that provided by the Secretary.

children.heading: Grasslands exception
children.label: ii Grasslands exception
children.text: In the case of grassland of special environmental significance, as determined by the Secretary, the Secretary may provide an amount not to exceed 75 percent of the fair market value of the agricultural land easement.

children.label: I
children.text: cash resources;

children.label: II
children.text: a charitable donation or qualified conservation contribution (as defined in ) from the private landowner from which the agricultural land easement will be purchased;section 170(h) of title 26

children.label: III
children.text: costs associated with securing a deed to the agricultural land easement, including the cost of appraisal, survey, inspection, and title; and

children.label: IV
children.text: other costs, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Criteria
children.label: A Criteria
children.text: The Secretary shall establish evaluation and ranking criteria to maximize the benefit of Federal investment under the program.

children.label: i
children.text: protecting agricultural uses and related conservation values of the land; and

children.label: ii
children.text: maximizing the protection of areas devoted to agricultural use.

children.label: i
children.text: meet the purposes of the program; and

children.label: ii
children.text: continue to maximize the benefit of the Federal investment under the program.

children.heading: Priority
children.label: D Priority
children.text: In evaluating applications under the program, the Secretary may give priority to an application for the purchase of an agricultural land easement that, as determined by the Secretary, maintains agricultural viability.

children.heading: Bidding down
children.label: E Bidding down
children.text: If the Secretary determines that 2 or more applications for cost-share assistance are comparable in achieving the purpose of the program, the Secretary shall not assign a higher priority to any of those applications solely on the basis of lesser cost to the program.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall enter into agreements with eligible entities to stipulate the terms and conditions under which the eligible entity is permitted to use cost-share assistance provided under this section.

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of an eligible entity certified under the process described in paragraph (5), a minimum of five years; and

children.label: ii
children.text: for all other eligible entities, at least three, but not more than five years.

children.label: i
children.text: are consistent with the purposes of the program;

children.label: ii
children.text: permit effective enforcement of the conservation purposes of such easements;

children.label: I
children.text: may be used only if the terms and conditions of the easement are not enforced by the eligible entity; and

children.label: AA
children.text: the holder of the easement fails to provide monitoring reports in a timely manner; or

children.label: BB
children.text: the Secretary has a reasonable and articulable belief that the terms and conditions of the easement have been violated; and

children.label: bb
children.text: prior to the inspection, the Secretary notifies the eligible entity and the landowner of the inspection and provides a reasonable opportunity for the eligible entity and the landowner to participate in the inspection;

children.label: iv
children.text: include a conservation plan only for any portion of the land subject to the agricultural land easement that is highly erodible cropland; and

children.label: v
children.text: include a limit on the impervious surfaces to be allowed that is consistent with the agricultural activities to be conducted.

children.heading: Additional permitted terms and conditions
children.label: D Additional permitted terms and conditions
i
children.text: are intended to keep the land subject to the agricultural land easement under the ownership of a farmer or rancher, as determined by the Secretary;

children.label: aa
children.text: has a limited and localized impact;

children.label: bb
children.text: does not harm the agricultural use and conservation values of the land subject to the easement;

children.label: cc
children.text: does not materially alter or affect the existing topography;

children.label: AA
children.text: includes a plan for the remediation of impacts to the agricultural use and conservation values of the land subject to the easement; and

children.label: BB
children.text: is approved by the Secretary prior to the initiation of mineral development activity;

children.label: ee
children.text: is not accomplished by any surface mining method;

children.label: ff
children.text: is within the impervious surface limits of the easement under subparagraph (C)(v); and

children.label: gg
children.text: uses practices and technologies that minimize the duration and intensity of impacts to the agricultural use and conservation values of the land subject to the easement; and

children.label: II
children.text: each area impacted by the subsurface mineral development shall be reclaimed and restored by the holder of the mineral rights at cessation of operation; and

children.label: iii
children.text: include other relevant activities relating to the agricultural land easement, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Substitution of qualified projects
children.label: E Substitution of qualified projects
children.text: An agreement shall allow, upon mutual agreement of the parties, substitution of qualified projects that are identified at the time of the proposed substitution.

children.label: i
children.text: the Secretary may terminate the agreement; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the Secretary may require the eligible entity to refund all or part of any payments received by the entity under the program, with interest on the payments as determined appropriate by the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: directly certify eligible entities that meet established criteria;

children.label: ii
children.text: enter into long-term agreements with certified eligible entities;

children.label: iii
children.text: accept proposals for cost-share assistance for the purchase of agricultural land easements throughout the duration of such agreements; and

children.label: iv
children.text: allow a certified eligible entity to use its own terms and conditions, notwithstanding paragraph (4)(C), as long as the terms and conditions are consistent with the purposes of the program.

children.label: I
children.text: a plan for administering easements that is consistent with the purpose of the program;

children.label: II
children.text: the capacity and resources to monitor and enforce agricultural land easements; and

children.label: aa
children.text: the long-term integrity of agricultural land easements on eligible land;

children.label: bb
children.text: timely completion of acquisitions of such easements; and

children.label: cc
children.text: timely and complete evaluation and reporting to the Secretary on the use of funds provided under the program;

children.label: I
children.text: been accredited by the Land Trust Accreditation Commission, or by an equivalent accrediting body, as determined by the Secretary;

children.label: II
children.text: acquired not fewer than 10 agricultural land easements under the program or any predecessor program; and

children.label: III
children.text: successfully met the responsibilities of the eligible entity under the applicable agreements with the Secretary, as determined by the Secretary, relating to agricultural land easements that the eligible entity has acquired under the program or any predecessor program; or

children.label: I
children.text: acquired not fewer than 10 agricultural land easements under the program or any predecessor program; and

children.label: II
children.text: successfully met the responsibilities of the eligible entity under the applicable agreements with the Secretary, as determined by the Secretary, relating to agricultural land easements that the eligible entity has acquired under the program or any predecessor program.

children.heading: Review
children.label: i Review
children.text: The Secretary shall conduct a review of eligible entities certified under subparagraph (A) every three years to ensure that such entities are meeting the criteria established under subparagraph (B).

children.label: I
children.text: allow the certified eligible entity a specified period of time, at a minimum 180 days, in which to take such actions as may be necessary to meet the criteria; and

children.label: II
children.text: revoke the certification of the eligible entity, if, after the specified period of time, the certified eligible entity does not meet such criteria.
permanent easements; or
easements for the maximum duration allowed under applicable State laws.

children.heading: Technical assistance
children.label: d Technical assistance
children.text: The Secretary may provide technical assistance, if requested, to assist in compliance with the terms and conditions of easements.
